chunks.text:
Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum publishes its first annual plan of work
Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum publishes its first annual plan of work
URL copied
-->
Online safety
Illegal and harmful content
News and updates
News
Published:
10 March 2021
Last updated:
10 March 2024
The Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum (DRCF) has today outlined its priorities for the coming year, marking a step-change in how regulation is coordinated across digital and online services.
The DRCF was formed by Ofcom, the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) and the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) in July 2020. Building on the strong working relationships between these organisations, the DRCF was established to ensure a greater level of cooperation between them, given the unique challenges posed by regulating online platforms.
Today’s DRCF
workplan for 2021/22 (PDF, 466.2 KB)
sets out a roadmap for how Ofcom, the CMA and the ICO will increase the scope and scale of their cooperation. This will involve bringing together their expertise and resources, working more closely on online regulatory matters of mutual importance, and reporting on results annually.
We are inviting comments and discussion on the DRCF’s plan of work and priorities for the year ahead. These should be submitted to
drcf@ofcom.org.uk
.
Ofcom to regulate harmful content online
The Government has decided to appoint Ofcom as the regulator for online harms in the UK.
Safer Internet Day 2021 – coming together for a better internet
Today is Safer Internet Day 2021. Promoting responsible and respectful use of digital technology, with the aim of creating a better internet for everybody.
A joined-up approach to digital regulation
The Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum (DRCF) today outlined its priorities for the coming year, marking a step-change in coordination of regulation across digital and online services.
